title: Tidal Memory
description: A brooding seascape where deep indigo waves collapse into foam that glows like scattered moonlight. The horizon line dissolves, leaving the viewer unsure where ocean ends and sky begins.

title: Rust & Reverie
medium: Acrylic and iron oxide on panel
description: Warm terracotta fields interrupted by veins of metallic rust, evoking abandoned industrial landscapes slowly reclaimed by wildflowers. Texture built through layered palette-knife work.

title: Still Life with Persimmons
description: Three persimmons rest on a rumpled linen cloth, their translucent orange skins catching afternoon light from an unseen window. A quiet meditation on patience and ripeness.

title: Ghosts of Magnolia
description: Towering magnolia branches rendered in near-monochrome lavender and grey, their blossoms floating like pale apparitions against a storm-dark background. The largest piece in the Botanical Ghosts series.

title: Soft Armour
medium: Acrylic and gold leaf on canvas
description: Overlapping shapes in muted rose and slate suggest both petals and plate armour. Flashes of gold leaf catch the light at unpredictable angles, lending the surface a living shimmer.

title: February Table
description: A half-eaten pomegranate, a ceramic mug of cold tea, and a dog-eared paperback share a weathered oak table. Morning light pools on the wood grain in soft geometric shapes.

title: Undertow
description: Dark cerulean currents pull across the canvas in sweeping horizontal strokes, punctuated by flecks of seafoam white. The painting hums with restrained energy, like the moment before a wave breaks.

title: Portrait of a Garden, Sleeping
description: A winter garden rendered in charcoal skeleton lines overlaid with thin washes of dusky violet oil. Dormant stems arc like calligraphy against a pale grey ground.

title: Ember Study No. 3
description: Part of a series exploring colour temperature at dusk. Cadmium orange and burnt sienna radiate outward from a concentrated core, fading to ash at the edges.

title: The Hours Between
description: A liminal interior scene — an unmade bed, curtains half-drawn, blue-grey pre-dawn light seeping across the floorboards. Neither night nor morning, the painting captures the space between sleep and waking.

title: Salt Marsh, Low Tide
description: Expansive mudflats stretch toward a pale lemon horizon, criss-crossed by silver channels of receding water. The palette is deliberately restrained — ochre, pewter, and the faintest blush of pink.

title: Inheritance
medium: Oil and found fabric on panel
description: A painted portrait layered over strips of vintage floral fabric sourced from estate sales. The sitter's face emerges through pattern and texture, suggesting identity as something assembled from the past.
